qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: JWT signature verification bypass in VerifyHostToken allows unauthenticated host impersonation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns VerifyHostToken accepting invalid host JWT signatures and using forged host identity for endpoints such as GET /api/v1/host. The advisory concerns platform-user access to GET /api/extclients/{network} and GET /api/nodes/{network} returning unfiltered WireGuard private keys without ownership-based response filtering.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The only final qualifying finding is an authentication bypass in VerifyHostToken, while the advisory is an authorization/data-filtering flaw where platform-user requests to GET /api/extclients/{network} or GET /api/nodes/{network} receive full WireGuard records including private keys.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-4hgg-c4rr-6h7f describes platform-user role access to GET /api/extclients/{network} and GET /api/nodes/{network} returning private keys without ownership-based filtering.
The judge input manifest lists jwt_host_token_signature_bypass as the only final qualifying_vuln finding.

jwt_host_token_signature_bypass/vulnerability_report.json identifies logic/jwts.go VerifyHostToken checking token != nil instead of token.Valid, which is a distinct host-token signature validation root cause.
